title: Question: AstNode::prettyName performance
body:
---


Author Name: **Johan Bjork**
Original Redmine Message: 1975 from https://www.veripool.org



---

Hi Wilson (and others)

A large portion of our Verilation runtime is spent calling AstNode::prettyName. Looking at the code I suspect it was originally intended to be used mostly for user output, errors and vcd tracing, but it's now used in a couple of places hitting every variable in a project multiple times.

There are a few cases where it's used
1) Matching against an input variable specified on the command line (ie, --clk/--no-clk and --unused-regexp).
2) Tracing (Called twice for each variable)
3) Misc usages in debug messages and various non-standard output formats (xml output etc etc)

I don't see any obvious ways to optimize prettyName, and the most temping solution would be to reduce the times it's called, however, the transformation from the internal name to the pretty name is not 1:1 so doing an "internalName" to solve (1) is not straight forward. Any ideas if this could be changed to be a 1:1 transformation?
For tracing it would be trivial to fix it to only call prettyName once for each variable, but I'd rather solve the entire problem at once. If making the prettyName output 1:1 is not possible, one other option could be to cache the value of prettyName in the AstNode's, but no other nodes do this.

Any ideas?

Thanks
/Johan
